title: pyrus aucuparia subsp. fenenkiana
intro:
Pyrus aucuparia subsp. fenenkiana, also known as the mountain ash, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues.

The health benefits of Pyrus aucuparia subsp. fenenkiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as a diuretic, making it a popular remedy for treating colds, flu, and urinary tract infections.

The herbal preparations of Pyrus aucuparia subsp. fenenkiana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
